message:
test(fj-conformance): add 31 oracle tests for Square primitive

Add a fresh oracle suite for the Square primitive following the
established per-primitive harness pattern in fj-conformance/tests.
Sister suites: cbrt_oracle, reciprocal_oracle, rsqrt_oracle,
sign_oracle (all landed earlier this week).

Each test calls eval_primitive(Primitive::Square, &[input], &{})
and asserts the result is bit-for-bit what x*x produces, exercising
both the numeric paths and the special-case f64 paths (NaN, inf,
signed zero). Covers:

I64 path (8 tests)
- 0, 1, 2, 3, 10
- -1, -2, -10
  (Square is sign-odd: square(-x) == square(x), pinned per case.)

F64 path (~13 tests)
- 0.0, +1.0, +2.0, +3.0, +0.5, +1e10
- -1.0, -2.0, -1e10
- f64::INFINITY, f64::NEG_INFINITY (square == +inf in both cases)
- f64::NAN (NaN propagates through square)
- f64::MIN_POSITIVE (smallest normal float — square is subnormal)

F32 path (small set)
- mirrors the f64 path at f32 precision so the dtype-promotion
  branch is exercised.

Identity & shape (rest of the suite)
- 1-D vector input + 2-D rank-2 input — pin that Square is
  element-wise and shape-preserving (no reduction, no transpose).
- The square(x) == x*x identity is asserted explicitly via
  eval_primitive(Primitive::Mul, &[x, x], &{}) on the same input
  and checking equality of the two outputs, so a regression in
  either Square OR Mul breaks the test.

Square is a small primitive, so 31 tests is generous coverage —
the value is in pinning the f64 special cases and the identity
contract, not in counting cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
